meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Stuck Apps (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Learn how to integrate analytics to overcome the 'stuck with basic apps' phase. A student-focused guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To move beyond basic projects, you must implement event-driven analytics. By tracking user behavior patterns, you transform 'static code' into 'data-informed products.' Use Antigravity to scaffold your tracking layer and Claude Code to automate the implementation, turning your portfolio into a measurable, professional-grade SaaS application.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks pushing pixels and debugging logic, but your app feels like a ghost town. You are stuck in the 'basic app' loop: build, deploy, hear crickets. The difference between a hobby project and a professional SaaS isn't just the code—it’s the visibility into how users actually interact with your creation.

Today, we break that cycle. By treating your code as a data-collection engine, you gain the clarity needed to iterate with purpose. We will leverage Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to implement robust event tracking, turning your portfolio from a stagnant file into a dynamic, performance-driven asset.

steps.title: Define Your Metrics Strategy
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the 'north star' metric—the one action that proves your app provides value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Suggest 5 high-impact user events to track for a [SaaS Type] application to validate user retention and feature adoption.
steps.claude_code_command: /ask Based on my current project structure, generate a plan for integrating PostHog or Mixpanel for these 5 events.

steps.title: Scaffold the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Create a centralized analytics utility to avoid polluting your component logic with third-party SDK calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust 'AnalyticsService' class in TypeScript that handles event tracking, user identification, and conditional logging for development environments.
steps.claude_code_command: /run Create a new file src/services/analytics.ts and export a singleton instance of the AnalyticsService.

steps.title: Instrument Critical User Flows
steps.description: Hook the service into your main UI components to start capturing real-time usage data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom React hook 'useAnalytics' that wraps the AnalyticsService and provides a clean interface for tracking clicks and page views.
steps.claude_code_command: /run Add useAnalytics hook to the Login and Dashboard components.

steps.title: Automate Data Validation
steps.description: Ensure your tracking implementation is bug-free by creating automated tests for your event payloads.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest unit tests for the AnalyticsService to verify that events are correctly formatted and sent to the provider.
steps.claude_code_command: /run Create src/tests/analytics.test.ts and verify event payload structure.

steps.title: Build a Data-Driven Dashboard
steps.description: Create an internal view to visualize the data you are collecting, proving your app's efficacy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a simple dashboard component that displays the top 3 user events tracked by the AnalyticsService.
steps.claude_code_command: /run Implement a basic AnalyticsDashboard component and mount it to the /admin route.

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus on 'conversion events' to keep your data signal-to-noise ratio high.
Use a proxy to send analytics data to avoid ad-blockers stripping your tracking scripts.
Implement 'Identity Resolution' early so you can track users across sessions, not just individual clicks.
Always include a 'debug mode' flag in your analytics service to log events to the console during development.
Track the time-to-value (TTV) metric—how long it takes a new user to complete their first core action.
content.next_steps: After verifying your events in the dashboard, perform a 'user testing' session with 5 friends. Use the data collected to identify exactly where they get confused, then use Claude Code to refactor those specific UI bottlenecks.
content.conversion_block: Your portfolio deserves to be backed by data, not just lines of code. Transition from a student developer to a product engineer by mastering the feedback loop today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
